texts.full_text: analytic expression for the photoluminescence spectral function of a model polariton system, which describes a quantum dot, with a finite number of fermionic levels, strongly interacting with the lowest photon mode of a pillar microcavity. Energy eigenvalues and wavefunctions of the electron-hole-photon system are obtained by numerically diagonalizing the Hamiltonian. Pumping and photon losses through the cavity mirrors are described with a master equation, which is solved in order to determine the stationary density matrix. The photon first-order correlation function, from which the spectral function is found, is computed with the help of the Quantum Regression Theorem. The spectral function qualitatively describes the polariton lasing regime in the model, corresponding to pumping rates two orders of magnitude lower than those needed for ordinary (photon) lasing. The second-order coherence functions for the photon and the electron-hole subsystems are computed as functions of the pumping rate.' author: - 'Carlos Andrés Vera$^{(a)}$, Herbert Vinck-Posada$^{(a)}$, and Augusto González$^{(b)}$' title: | Polariton lasing in a multilevel quantum dot\ strongly coupled to a single photon mode --- Introduction {#sec1} ============ Excitonic polaritons are quasi-particles that result from the strong coupling between cavity photons and exciton excitations of semiconductor materials. texts.full_text: led ovarian hyperstimulation and early pregnancy in women exposed to assisted reproduction. Female sex hormones have vasorelaxing effects in non-pregnant and pregnant women. We aimed to investigate the effect of controlled ovarian hyperstimulation (COH) for in vitro fertilization (IVF), and early pregnancy, on arterial stiffness as assessed by digital pulse wave analysis (DPA), hypothesizing reduced arterial stiffness as an effect of increased estrogen levels. A total of 68 women undergoing IVF were examined with DPA before conception and during IVF treatment with COH and embryo transfer (ET), and in gestational week seven in 19 women who became pregnant. Heart rate (HHeart rate (HR) was recorded, along with other cardiovascular parameters such as augmentation index (AIx), a marker of arterial stiffness that is influenced by heart rate among other factors. Results showed no significant changes in AIx during COH compared to baseline levels before the start of IVF treatment. However, there were notable decreases in AIx observed from pre-IVF values at gestational week seven for those who became pregnant (p < 0.05). This suggests that while controlled ovarian hyperstimulation does not significantly alter arterial stiffness as measured by DPA and represented through changes in the augmentation index during treatment cycles, early pregnancy is associated with a reduction in AIx. The decrease in AIx observed during early pregnancy could be attributed to increased estrogen levels known for their vasodilatory effects. Estrogen can improve endothelial function and reduce vascular resistance which may contribute to reduced arterial stiffness seen at this stage of gestation despite the elevated cardiac output typically experienced by pregnant women. In conclusion, while COH in IVF cycles does not appear to affect arterial stiffness as measured through DPA parameters like AIx, early pregnancy is associated with a significant reduction. This finding underscores the complex interplay between reproductive hormones and cardiovascular function during assisted reproduction processes leading into successful pregnancies. Further research could explore longer-term effects on maternal vascular health throughout full term gestation in women undergoing IVF treatments versus natural conceptions to bet

Recently, Shared Medical Technology, a small, rural-based business in Rice Lake, Wis., was successful in winning a $3.2 million grant from the National Institutes of Health’s SBIR program to construct a prototype a portable, low-cost diagnostic device for early detection of sepsis. This funding not only advanced their technology but also created local jobs, attracted talent to the rural area, and positioned the company as a leader in medical innovation. By leveraging federal R&D dollars, Shared Medical Technology transformed a concept into a market-ready solution, demonstrating the tangible benefits of programs like SBIR and STTR. For states and communities, such success stories underscore the importance of fostering partnerships between government, academic institutions, and private enterprises. texts.full_text: hey told us you can't go outside' Published: 10/08/2013 at 1:01 PM Text smaller Text bigger Authorities used “Gestapo tactics” on senior citizens visiting Yellowstone National Park, locking them inside their hotel under “armed guard” during the Obama administration’s government shutdown, according to a guide. Officials even refused to allow the tourists to stop for bathroom breaks during the two-and-a-half hour drive out of the park, he said. Gordon Hodgson spoke in a telephone interview with the Livinston, Mont.news station KRLD about his experience leading an elder tour group through Yellowstone when President Obama ordered all nonessential government services closed on Oct. 1 due to Congress’ failure to pass legislation funding federal operations past Sept. 30. The National Park Service (NPS), which manages the park, implemented measures that left some visitors feeling trapped and intimidated by authorities' actions during this period of furlough for many government employees. Hodgson described how NPS officials abruptly changed their policy toward tourists staying at Yellowstone’s Old Faithful Snow Lodge & Cabins mid-tour on Thursday night, effectively locking down the facility with no advance notice

texts.full_text: e red cent. Small wonder that blogging has become a force of mainstream media. Related Files & Links Open File DOWNLOAD Services Litigation November 3, 2008 By: Joel Cohen Since the days of John Stuart Mill, we have believed in the free "marketplace of ideas" — that is, the constitutional right for all people to share their thoughts, even stridently unpopular ones, with the rest of the world. Historically, however, we have entrusted that ability to journalists and other media commentators. And, absent extraordInary circumstances, the courts have frowned on "prior restraints" of such comments. Yes, the crazies at Speakers' Corner in London, or similar venues, have likewise been able to weigh in for many years. But their often provocative comments are typically limited to a finite audience within earshot of that Hyde Park "broadcast." Nowadays, though, cyberspace enables anyone willing to spring for a domain name and pay an Internet service provider (ISP) $15 a month to become a "publisher." And even better for these latter-day Horace Greeleys, they can corral a limitless number of "reporters" without paying one red ce

Kento turned, slowing down as Cye and Rowen caught up. "You're in a hurry today. Already thinking about lunch?" Cye inquired, a smirk playing on his lips. "You know Mia's lunch. I can't wait to dig in. I hope it's the chicken salad sandwich. I've been thinking about it since this morning," Kento replied, his eyes lighting up in anticipation. "You've been thinking about a sandwich since this morning? Kento, you've got to broaden your lunch horizons," Rowen chimed in, his eyes widening in mock surprise. "Hey, it's a sandwich, a lunch, a Mia's lunch. It's a big deal," Kento defended, a playful scuffle about to ensue. As they reached the courtyard, they saw Mia, Kento's sister, sitting at the courtyard's big, round, stone table, lunch spread out. It was a table they often occupied, a courtyard they'd made their lunch-time territory. Mia, a big, big sister, was a lunch-time hero, a culinary delight, a delight Kento's friends often envied. "There's Mia. It's a lunch-time, friends. Let's dig in. Mia, what's in the lunch-time box today?" Kento's voice was a mixture of excitement, a mixture of a growling, a mixture of a lunch-time symphony. "There's a lunch of a variety today, Kento. Migration Background: Children from immigrant families are likely to experience mental health problems compared to those without a migration background to potential stressors related to acculturation, language barriers, or social integration difficulties. This finding underscores the need for culturally sensitive support and intervention programs tailored specifically towards migrants' needs within preschool settings in Bavaria. 4. Environmental Factors: The study also finds a correlation between living conditions (e.g., overcrowded housing, poor sanitation) and mental health status among the children surveyed; those residing under substandard environmental circumstances display elevated rates of psychological distress compared to their more privileged counterparts in better-equipped homes or neighborhoods conducive for play and learning activities outdoors. 5. The PM said that this day is not only about remembering the sacrifices of those valiant men but also an occasion for us as citizens, irrespective or religion caste creed color class to reaffirm commitment towards national security. He emphasized on unity among all Pakistanis and called upon everyone including youth students professionals from every walk life contribute positively in making our country strong. He further stated that it is imperative we continue working together under the leadership of President Mamnoon Hussain & PM Shahid Khaqan Abbasi to build a prosperous nation where peace prevails. texts.full_text: We consider five distinct models that differ in their update mode (synchronous or asynchronous), coupling strength ($J=0.2$ or $1.0$), and local map properties (unimodal or bimodal). For each model, we compute the autocorrelation function $C(t)$ of the order parameter and analyze its early-time decay to extract estimates of $\theta''$ and $z$. Our results indicate that the update mode has a significant impact on the dynamic universality classes exhibited by these systems. address: | $^1$ Department of Physics, Graduate School of Science, Kyoto University, Kyoto 606, Japan\ $^2$ CEA — Service de Physique de l’Etat Condensé, Centre d’Etudes de Saclay, 91191 Gif-sur-Yvette, France author: - Philippe Marcq$^1$ and Hugues Chaté$^2$ date: 'September 19, 1997' title: 'Early-time critical dynamics of lattices of coupled chaotic maps' ---

texts.full_text: For more information, visit National Multiple Sclerosis Society. Myocardial Infarction: A myocardial infarction is another term for heart attack, or a blockage of blood flow to the heart. For more information, visit the American Heart Association. Palliative Care: Palliative care is treatment for seriously ill patients that includes medical care to manage pain and symptoms, as well as emotional and practical support. Palliative care teams can include doctors and nurses, social workers, nutritionists, spiritual advisors and in-home caregivers. During palliative care, healthcare professionals continue to try to cure the disease. Parkinson's Disease: Parkinson’s disease is a neurodegenerative disorder that affects neurons in a specific area of the brain, resulting in issues with movement and often starts with tremors in one hand. For more information, visit Parkinson’s Foundation. Personal Care: Personal care is a broad term that supports basic personal hygiene and activities of daily living, including dressing, grooming, and toileting. Comfort Keepers’ trained caregivers assist with personal care needs ranging from stand-by assistance to full care depending on each client’s individual needs. This can include basic tasks, such as bathing, mobility assistance, incontinence and toileting care, preparing and serving meals, to more hands-on personal care including position changes for those clients that are bedbound and specialized care for those with memory issues or cognitive impairments, fall risk assessment and prevention, change in condition monitoring. Personal home care services can also include assistance with outings, social events, shopping trips, and other activities of interest to maintain engagement both physically and emotionally. texts.full_text: lost my loving dad quite suddenly. I'll never forget that day. I was washing dishes when the phone rang at 9:20 on Tuesday morning. I never expected to hear the news that I heard when I picked up that receiver. My sister was on the end of the line and I could barely hear her. She was sobbing. I asked her to repeat what she had said and she replied: "Dad just died." What?!!!! My heart dropped to the floor and I felt sick, dizzy, disoriented. Then I thought it was a really bad joke and told my sister that it wasn't funny. She told me that she would never joke about such a thing and thatit was true. I couldn't believe it. I felt like I was in a nightmare and I just wanted to wake up. But it wasn't a nightmare, it was my reality.

This result applies for instance to the system GJ 876: we reproduce the observed orbital elements through a fully hydrodynamical simulation of the evolution of the resonant planets.' address: - 'Institut für Astronomie & Astrophysik – Universität Túbingen The formation and stability of resonant planet pairs in exoplanetary systems are intriguing topics that have garnered significant attention from astrophysicists. The study delves into the complex dynamics governing these interactions, particularly focusing on how planets migrate within their host protoplanetary discs. ### Introduction Resonance plays a crucial role in shaping the architecture of planetary systems around other stars. When an outer planet migrates towards its star at sufficient speed and with appropriate inclination relative to any inner planets or disk material, it can capture these bodies into resonant configurations where their orbital periods are related by simple ratios (e.g., 2:1). This resonance condition is not static; as the outer planet continues inward migration, gravitational interactions between the pair cause their eccentricities and inclinations to oscillate. ### Migration and Resonance Capture The migration of planets within protoplanetary discs typically occurs due to a combination of aerodynamic drag from the surrounding gas and torques exerted by any companion objects. As an outer planet migrates inward, it can encounter an inner planet or perturbations caused by disk material that are already at resonance with its own orbit. ### Eccentricity Growth The capture into Mean Motion Resonance (MMR) is often accompanied by a significant increase in eccentricities of the involved planets. This is due to gravitational torques from each other and their host star, which can lead to chaotic orbits if no damping mechanism acts upon them. ### Damping Mechanisms To prevent such excessive eccentricity growth, various mechanisms have been proposed that could dampen orbital oscillations in resonant systems: 1. Tidal Forces: The tidal forces exerted by the host star on its planets

texts.full_text: the negative. Focus on the good. Be thankful that you have the ability to make a change, that you’re lucky enough to start moving in a new direction. 5. One change doesn’t mean everything has to change. Sometimes, it feels like one change will completely alter the direction of your life. However, some things are always going to remain the same. There are going to be certain constants. Maybe you’ll be living somewhere new, but you’ll have the same friends. Maybe you’ll be switching career paths, but you’ll still have your hobbies. You won’t be giving up literally everything at once. 6. You deserve to live your most authentic life. You might feel like everything is fine right now — but do you want things to remain exactly like this forever? If not, then you need to make a change. You don’t want the years to keep passing while you remain in the same place. You don’t want to keep procrastinating and never end up at your destination. It’s never going to feel like it’s the right time to make a change, but you have to do it if you want true happiness. You have to take a chance on yourself soon. texts.full_text: summary dismissal of a prior habeas corpus petition bars consideration of a subsequent petition. Melvin C. Dodson, the petitioner, was convicted in the Circuit Court of Stafford County of (1) damaging the Stafford County jail for the purpose of escaping (2) committing grand larceny while breaking and entering into the Stafford County jail; and (3) carrying a firearm during commission of the above offenses. He was sentenced to twenty years on each offense, with all sentences running concurrently. Petitioner appealed his convictions in this Court for abuse of discretion by the trial court's denial of his request that he be appointed counsel at appeal from these convictions because petitioner was indigent and not represented below. The Director argues that we have no jurisdiction to hear an original action on habeas corpus since it is a matter over which we have appellate jurisdiction. This argument is based upon the fact that Code § 15.2-400 authorizes appeals from circuit courts to this Court, but makes no mention of any right to appointed counsel at appeal. Is this going to be a garbage in, garbage out, or is this something that could potentially be a game-changer in terms of how people react to their own data and then bring more data into the interactions they have with care providers? Lee: It’s always a challenge to predict what the market is going to do, but I think that’s a remarkable statistic that you cited. My prediction is that the increased volume of person- generated data from mobile health devices is going to be a game-changer. This view also reflects how the Healthcare forum members (which includes members from Capgemini, Philips, IbM, Oracle and HP) view the future. The commercial demand for mobile medical devices, things that can be worn, embedded, or swallowed, as in pills, as you mentioned, is growing ever more. The software and the applications that will be developed to be used with the devices is going to grow by leaps and bounds. As you say, there are big players getting involved. Already some of the pedometer type devices that measure the number of steps taken in a day have captured the interest of many, many people. Even David Sedaris, serious guy that he is, was writing about it recently in ‘The New Yorker’. What we will find is that many of the health indicators that we used to have to go to the doctor or nurse or lab to get information on will become available to us through these remote devices. There will be a question, of course, as to reliability and validity of the information, to your point about garbage in, garbage out, but I think standards development will help here This, again, is where The Open Group comes in. texts.full_text: to this ‘Book’ Friday Jun 16, 2017 at 2:01 AM Ed Symkus More Content Now If you go to enough movies, it’s inevitable that sometimes you’re going to get lucky. You’re going to stumble into one that’s this funny and sweet and sad and serious. This one even crosses over into thriller territory. “The Book of Henry” is a movie that will take your emotions out for a ride. The title suggests that it’s about a book, and it is — a small, bright red one. But it’s more about the 11-year-old boy it belongs to, who is constantly writing and drawing in its blank pages. It wouldn’t be proper to call Henry (Jaeden Lieberher, from “St. Vincent” and “Midnight Special”) merely a bright kid. When the students in his elementary school classroom get up, one at a time, to talk about “My Legacy,” he launches into a brief speech about staving off existential crisis. After class, he’s outside on a pay phone, trading stocks. At home with his mom and his younger brother — dad left long ago — he tinkers with Rube Goldberg-like contraptions, keeps his eyes on the family’s financial dealings, and is always calculating about their future. This movie has a great emotional hook: It shows how Henry views himself as an adult. He thinks he can save everyone in this world from suffering by figuring out the best way to handle things — and that includes his own family, which is on edge because mom (Pete Postlethwaite) lost her job. texts.full_text: dermal fibroblast or mixed cell type groups. Further studies revealed that adipose cells upregulated TGF-β1 (transforming growth factor beta 1) expression. The increased levels of TGF-β1 were found to directly inhibit melanocyte differentiation and pigmentation, as confirmed by in vitro studies using recombinant human TGF-β1 on primary cultures of epidermal cells. However, blocking the action of TGF-β1 with a neutralizing antibody or siRNA targeting TGF-β1 reversed these effects, leading to increased expression of MITF and other pigmentation-related genes. This suggests that the inhibitory effect of adipose tissue-derived stromal cells on melanocyte differentiation is mediated at least in part by levels of TGF-β1. These findings provide new insights into how different types of dermal stromal cells can modulate skin pigmentation and may have implications for understanding conditions associated with altered skin, such as vitiligo or hyperpigmentation disorders. Furthermore, this knowledge can be applied to develop novel therapeutic strategies aimed at regulating melanocyte activity in the treatment of various dermatological conditions. texts.full_text: we will discuss how split SUSY can alleviate flavor changing neutral currents and CP violation in MSSM. Secondarily, our main focus lies on studying an important contribution at the leading order (LO) due to $W$ EDM within such a scenario which affects electric dipole moments of particles like electron or quarks. To set up this framework we start by introducing soft supersymmetry breaking terms relevant for split SUSY. These include scalar masses, gaugino mass parameters and trilinear couplings among them. For simplicity in our analysis hereafter we assume that all the sfermion (squark/slepton) are degenerate with common squared-mass $M_S^2$; soft breaking terms for Higgs sector contains $\mu$, B$\mu$. Similarly, gaugino masses have a universal form M_1/3 = m_{\lambda} where index 1 runs over the three SM gauge groups. For simplicity we assume that all these parameters are real and CP is conserved. In this setup trilinear couplings in superpotential can be written as A_i Y_i, here indices i run through quarks/leptons. The electric dipole moment (EDM) of a particle arises due to its interaction with an external electromagnetic field. This effect occurs when the CP is not conserved or if there exist non-zero EDMs in theory under consideration . In MSSM, one-loop contributions from chargino/neutralino sector and two loop Barr-Zee type diagrams involving Higgs bosons are known to contribute significantly towards electron (or neutron) EDM [2-4]. However these estimates depend on the mass of lightest CP-even higgs. The recent observation that a 125 GeV scalar is consistent with SM predictions has ruled out many such models which predict much larger values for Higgs masses. texts.full_text: and Key Stage 3 students aged between 9 – 13 years old. The ‘Fake News and the Media’ lesson has been created as a reaction to the current issues around the distortion and misuse of false information and the influence it is having on young people’s choices. As the lines continue to blur between what is a real news story and what is false, the interactive lessons will provide teachers and their students with the essential skills to navigate the news agenda safely online and across social media. Delivered by Madame Tussauds performers, the ‘Fake News and the Media’ lesson will lead students through a critique of how the media presents news whilst also analysing the difference between rumour, spin, satire and false information. It will include: Structured discussion around modern celebrity culture and the media, and how the two are linked. Working in groups to categorise news stories according to different criteria, including, how the news was shared;

To prepare pure thulium titanate nanostructures, the presence of starch and sonication treatment were concurrently obligatory. Therefore, sol-gel assisted sonochemical method can be used as a successful process for synthesis of thulium titanate nanostructures. According to the BET results, in the presence of ultrasound, the specific surface area of thulium titanate nanostructures increased, which can be attributed to the breaking of the nanostructures during the ultrasound treatment. This increased surface area can be beneficial for the sonophotocatalytic activity of the thulium titanate/polyaniline nanocomposites. The X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of the thulium titanate nanostructures confirmed the formation of the desired crystalline phases. The transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images further confirmed the formation of thulium titanate nanostructures. The energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) analysis showed the presence of thulium (Tm) and titanium (Ti) elements in the thulium titanate nanostructures. The Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy analysis showed the presence of characteristic peaks of thulium titanate and polyaniline in the thulium titanate/polyaniline nanocomposites. The ultraviolet-visible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy analysis showed the presence of polyaniline in the thulium titanate/polyaniline nanocomposites. texts.full_text: er 12-14 years. The aim of the present prospective study was to evaluate the long-term outcome of implants placed simultaneously with guided bone regeneration (GBR) using resorbable and non-resorbable membranes. The original study population consisted of 72 patients receiving a total of 265 implants. In all GBR-treated sites, demineralized bovine bone mineral (DBBM) was used in combination with either collagen or titanium-reinforced resorbable and non-resorbable membranes to achieve the regeneration necessary for implant placement. After a follow-up period of 12-14 years post-surgery, data were available from 58 patients (70 implants). The overall survival rate was reported at 96.3% with no significant differences observed between groups using resorbable versus non-resorbable membranes in terms of implant success or marginal bone level changes. The mean peri-implant marginal bone loss over the observation period did not exceed 1 mm, indicating a stable condition around most implants regardless of membrane type used during GBR. Soft tissue parameters such as probing depth and bleeding on probing were also within acceptable clinical ranges for both groups. Radiographic analysis revealed no significant differences in crestal bone levels between resorbable (mean loss: 0.8 ± 0.3 mm) and non-resorbable membranes (mean loss: 0.9 ± 0.4 mm). Patient satisfaction was high, with the majority reporting functional comfort and aesthetic outcomes that met or exceeded their expectations. The study concluded that both resorbable collagen and titanium-reinforced non-resorbable membranes in conjunction with DBBM are effective for GBR procedures leading to successful long-term implant integration when used as part of a comprehensive treatment plan. The choice between membrane types should be based on specific clinical scenarios, surgeon preference, and patient factors rather than concerns about the longevity or success rates associated with either material. This 12-14 year follow-up study provides valuable evidence supporting GBR techniques in dental implantology for achieving long-term stability and aesthetic outcomes des

texts.full_text: is asking if de factoSchengen countries (Andorra, Monaco, San Marino and Vatican City) are exempted or notfrom President Trump’s US travel ban which specifically targets the 26 Schengen signatories. They mention that travelers from these four small states will be affected by a separateban on people who have visited any of thoseSchengen countries within last14 days due to open borders, but they wonder if there's no way for authorities to verify their exact origin. My thought is the travel ban would likely apply in this scenario. Since Andorra, Monaco, San Marino and Vatican City are not officially partof Schengen Area yet share anopen border with it (with exception ofVatican which has a separate agreement), they could be considered as connected toSchengen countries under Trump’s order. In terms of verifying travel history from these states backto the US, authorities might rely on travelers' self-declaration and any available records. texts.full_text: hemoglobin polymerization and the formation of inflexible sickled erythrocytes. Accumulation of sickled erythrocytes in the microcirculation causes acute vaso-occlusive events that lead to pain and acute organ injury. Chronic arterial vasculopathy, with intimal proliferation and arterial stenosis, can lead to complications such as stroke and pulmonary hypertension. The etiology of arterial stenosis in sickle cell anemia is poorly understood. We hypothesize that intimal proliferation in sickle cell anemia isan adaptive response to recurrent arterial occlusion.
